commit.message:
fix(devcontainer): persist Claude auth across rebuilds with bind mount and machine-id

Revert from Docker volume to host bind mount for ~/.claude, and restore
project-specific /etc/machine-id generation. Empty machine-id was the
root cause of re-authentication on rebuild.

Closes #8

commit.message:
fix(devcontainer): generate project-specific machine-id

Generate /etc/machine-id from project folder name hash to maintain
consistent container identity across rebuilds while ensuring different
projects have different IDs.

Format: md5sum of "devcontainer-${localWorkspaceFolderBasename}"

commit.message:
fix(devcontainer): create .claude.json to prevent auth re-confirmation

Add creation of ~/.claude/.claude.json in postCreateCommand.
Without this file, Claude Code treats the environment as a new
installation and requests re-authentication on every container rebuild.

Refs #8

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Claude/fix level progression bug mx6 bm (#344)

* Add Illness Log as dedicated tool and integrate into workout decisions

1. ExploreTab: Added Illness Log as its own tool in the Body category
   (was previously buried as a keyword under Injury Log). Full
   description surfaces the Neck Check protocol, daily check-ins, and
   return-to-training phases.

2. Daily Directive: Active illness now gates the "should I train today?"
   decision. If the illness engine says no training (fever, systemic,
   below-neck, or GI symptoms), the directive forces rest with
   illness-specific headline, actions, and training modifications.
   Recovering athletes get RPE caps and duration limits.

3. Performance Engine: Added illness as an 11th readiness factor
   (weight 0.10). Active illness scores 5/100 (triggers critical limiter
   cap), recovering scores proportional to allowed volume. Recently
   resolved illness (within 14 days) continues to impact readiness
   during return-to-training phases. Existing factor weights rebalanced
   to accommodate.

Science: Weidner 1998 Neck Check Rule, Friman & Wesslén 2000 fever/
myocarditis risk, Schwellnus 2016 return-to-play protocol.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FracW8c5FPGAWk1NcmX4LQ

* Fix next workout progression skipping back to W1/D1

The getNextWorkout logic (daily directive) and nextUpSession (WorkoutView)
both iterated weeks from W1 forward, returning the first uncompleted
session. If a user completed a later session (e.g. Deload Day 1) but
earlier sessions were uncompleted, it would jump back to W1/D1 instead
of continuing to Deload Day 2.

Fix: find the most recently completed session by log date, then search
forward from that position for the next uncompleted session. Only falls
back to "first uncompleted from start" for fresh mesocycles with no
completions.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FracW8c5FPGAWk1NcmX4LQ

* Fix mobile viewport overflow and pinch-to-zoom issues

- Lock viewport scale to 1 and disable user-scalable (standard PWA behavior)
- Remove max-w-lg from tab bar that constrained it unnecessarily
- Add overflow-x-hidden + w-full to main content container and overlay wrapper
- Change ExploreTab pinned tools from 4-col to 3-col grid for better mobile fit

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FracW8c5FPGAWk1NcmX4LQ

* Fix nutrition tab ignoring user's grappling intensity selection

The duration threshold (>=60min) was overriding the user's explicit
intensity choice, so a light 75-min flow session got classified as
"hard grappling" with 1.2x calorie multiplier. Now only hard_sparring,
competition_prep, or long moderate sessions (90+ min) trigger hard
grappling nutrition. Light sessions stay light regardless of duration.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FracW8c5FPGAWk1NcmX4LQ

---------

commit.message:
Make homepage and blog posts responsive for mobile

- Add missing viewport meta tag (root cause of mobile layout issues)
- Fix Netflix slider swipe: replace stale React state with refs for
  drag tracking (isDraggingRef, startXRef) so touch events work correctly
- Add touch swipe support to HeroCarousel (left/right swipe to navigate)
- Resize slider cards at ≤480px (175×99px) to show exactly 2 per screen
- Reduce excess vertical spacing between sliders on mobile: lower
  sliderContainer padding/margin and sliderWrapper min-height at small breakpoints
- Move sliders outer container padding to CSS class for responsive control
- Fix blog post: overflow-x auto on code blocks, better small-screen
  font sizes and quote mark positioning

refactor: collapse to 2-container architecture (backend monolith + dashboard)

Restructure from 5 separate app containers to 2:
- Single backend/ directory with Python packages (data_engine, intelligence,
  strategy, risk_execution, shared) in one FastAPI process
- All existing endpoints, WebSocket, DB schemas preserved at same paths
- Python 3.12, single Dockerfile for backend
- Docker Compose: backend + dashboard + infra (postgres, questdb, redis, nats)
- Dashboard proxy updated from 'api' to 'backend' service name

commit.message:
Remove -π/2 offset transform — use raw radians

Camera rotates with ship (camAngle + π/2), making the previous
transform incorrect. Raw offset now maps to screen position:
0=right, π/2=above, π=left, 3π/2=below ship.

commit.message:
feat: add application-load-balancer resource type (#29)

* feat: add application-load-balancer resource type

Adds support for YC Application Load Balancer (ALB) as a managed
resource type, enabling scheduled start/stop via Rundeck.

- yc_common.py: status constants ALB_ACTIVE/STOPPING/STOPPED/STARTING
- node_source.py: list_alb(), alb_to_node(), _ALB_STATUSES mapping
- start.py: start_alb() function and match case
- stop.py: stop_alb() function and match case
- tests: full unit test coverage (TestStartAlb, TestStopAlb, TestAlbToNode)
- e2e.yml: application_load_balancer input flag and test job
- examples/main.tf: ALB added to production example with stop_order=3
- README.md: application-load-balancer marked as done (0.3.0)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update .gitignore

* fix(e2e): pass --location to yc alb load-balancer create

ALB API requires at least one location in allocation_policy.
Resolve subnet_id from the same query used for network_id and
pass --location zone=...,subnet-id=... to satisfy the constraint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(e2e): increase ALB ACTIVE wait timeout to 10 minutes

ALB provisioning takes longer than NLB. 30×10s was not enough;
extend to 60×10s (10 min) to avoid false timeouts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(e2e): increase ALB delete verification timeout to 10 minutes

ALB deletion takes longer than NLB. 18×10s (3 min) was insufficient;
extend to 60×10s (10 min).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: start polling in simulated mode so simulator UI shows activity

The polling loop that fetches simulator state, level maps, trades, and
diagnostics was gated on Tradovate connection only. When running on
simulated data without Tradovate, the UI never polled — so the simulator
appeared inactive despite running in the background.

- Start polling on mount if shadow trading is enabled (even without Tradovate)
- Start polling when shadow toggle is turned on
- Restart polling on symbol change in simulated mode
- Stop polling when shadow is disabled and Tradovate is not connected
- Bump version to v1.17.5

commit.message:
Hotfix v0.4.9 — fix tools hanging on Telegram/Discord/Slack (#570)

* Fix memory tools hanging on Telegram/Discord/Slack (#569)

* fix(agent): always bypass permissions in headless mode

The Claude SDK backend only activated bypassPermissions when the
bypass_permissions setting was explicitly true (defaults to false).
Without it, tool calls needing approval (like Bash — used for memory
save, web search, etc.) hang indefinitely on messaging channels
(Telegram, Discord, Slack) because there's no terminal to show the
permission prompt. The agent then reports "trouble accessing memory
tools" because the tool call never completes.

PocketPaw always runs headless — the PreToolUse security hook still
blocks dangerous commands regardless of permission mode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add regression tests for headless permission bypass

6 tests verify the fix works:
- Source inspection confirms permission_mode is unconditional
- Subprocess round-trip: remember → recall via CLI dispatcher
- Timeout guard catches the hang bug (5s limit)

Tests fail on the old code (conditional bypass) and pass on the fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: bump version to 0.4.9

Hotfix release — fixes tool permissions hanging on messaging channels.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Full foundation for APEX AI quantitative trading system:

  risk_state, trades, calibration, predictions) auto-created via init SQL

  Lightweight Charts candlestick, Recharts equity curve, Zustand state, WebSocket feed

  API service, and dashboard — all with health checks
- Shared Python utilities: asyncpg pool, QuestDB ILP client, Redis async,
  NATS JetStream with auto-stream creation, Pydantic settings

commit.message:
fix(video-swap): prevent Bull job stalling and fix I2V base64 encoding

- Add lockDuration (10min), stalledInterval (5min), maxStalledCount to
  VIDEO_SWAP queue — Modal calls take 5-15min, default 30s lock caused
  stalling
- Add extendLock() calls between each pipeline step to keep lock fresh
- Convert R2 URL to base64 data URL before sending to Modal I2V endpoint
  — Modal cannot reach R2 directly, was causing PIL.UnidentifiedImageError
- Add E2E and integration test scripts for full pipeline validation

feat(ENG-32): knowledge graph query-time traversal for relational recall

Add GraphRecallService that extracts entity names from queries using
simple NER heuristics, searches the knowledge graph for matching entities,
and retrieves memories attached to those entities and their 1-hop neighbors.

Wire graph recall into the MemoryQueryService recall pipeline so vector
search results are merged with graph-sourced memories, with a 1.2x score
boost for memories appearing in both result sets. Feature-gated behind
GRAPH_RETRIEVAL_ENABLED env var (default false).
